name: Tyumen Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the snows of the northern reaches of the oblast
the Arctic Ocean to the north and the rivers Ob and Irtysh
the taiga forests and farmland of the southern oblast
the courage and labour of the people; the historical struggle to settle and develop Siberia
the gold crowns of the historic coats of arms of the three constituent regions

description: A horizontal triband of white, blue and green with a red isoceles pile based on the hoist and three yellow crowns arranged in a row in the upper hoist, on the white stripe.
history: The flag of Tyumen Oblast was adopted on 24 May 1995. The three crowns symbolise the south of the oblast together with the two autonomous okrugs (Khanty-Mansi and Yamalo-Nenets) that historically formed part of it.

features.symbolism: the white-blue-green tricolour represents the snowy north, the rivers and seas, and the southern taiga and steppes of the vast Tyumen Oblast.
the courage and labour of the people and the historical struggle to settle and develop Siberia
represent the three constituent regions of the historical Tyumen Oblast: the south, the Khanty-Mansi Autonomous Okrug and the Yamalo-Nenets Autonomous Okrug.
